pages.text:
HB25-1001
A BILL FOR AN ACT
CONCERNING THE REGULATION OF EMISSIONS FROM INDUSTRIAL FACILITIES.

BY REPRESENTATIVE(S) Smith, Garcia, Thompson
BY SENATOR(S) Williams

Committee on Energy & Environment

Bill Summary
The bill modifies Colorado law and creates deterministic sample
content for Project Geode parser, entity, graph, and tagging tests.

BE IT ENACTED BY THE GENERAL ASSEMBLY OF THE STATE OF COLORADO:

SECTION 1.
25-7-114.7, Colorado Revised Statutes, is amended to read:
(1) The division shall establish emission monitoring requirements for each industrial facility that operates a stationary source.
(2) As used in this section:
(a) "stationary source" means a building, structure, facility, or installation that emits or may emit an air pollutant.
(b) The House Energy & Environment Committee reviewed the industrial emissions reporting schedule.
(3) The commission shall adopt rules in accordance with § 24-4-103, C.R.S.
(4) A person who knowingly falsifies an emissions report commits a class 1 misdemeanor and is subject to a penalty of $10,000.

SECTION 2.
25-7-114.8, Colorado Revised Statutes, is amended to read:
(1) The division shall maintain an industrial compliance fund.
(2) The fund consists of fees, gifts, grants, and $2,500,000 transferred for the 2025-26 fiscal year.

(3) A facility shall submit a compliance plan by July 1, 2025.

SECTION 3.
25-8-205, Colorado Revised Statutes, is amended BY THE ADDITION OF A NEW SUBSECTION to read:
(7) A permit for an industrial discharge must include monitoring conditions for water quality impacts.
(8) For the 2025-26 state fiscal year, $500,000 is appropriated from the General Fund to the department of public health and environment for implementation.

SECTION 4. Effective date.
This act takes effect July 1, 2025.
